ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Heather Volante for Co-Executors.

Ms. Volante reported that there is a balance of $1,208.18 left from litigation counsel Edward Koplowitz and that there is no outstanding balance by the accountant. Court indicated that some of the extraordinary fees requested were for ordinary and not extraordinary services. Ms. Volante explained in detail of services that she has provided. The Court took the request for statutory fees and extraordinary fees under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Petition Of Co-Executoris To Authorize And Instruct The Co-Executors Concerning The Claims Of Inheritance Funding Company, Inc.
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Continued To Sep-25-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Settlement Efforts
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Continued To Sep-25-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Koplowitz and Heather Volante for Deanna Boyd-Colon and Monique Mervin (Co-Executors). Steven Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company.

Mr. Koplowitz reported that they have had fruitful and productive settlement discussions, and requested that the hearing dates on the evidentiary hearing and the Petition for Instructions be continued. The evidentiary hearing on the assignments is continued from September 13, 2013 and September 20, 2013, to October 25, 2013 and November 1, 2013 (at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204). The Petition for Instructions is continued from August 6, 2013 to September 25, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on settlement efforts is also continued to September 25, 2013. Ms. Volante is to amend the petition for final distribution. If settlement is reached, Mr. Koplowitz will file a Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution and the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement will have the same hearing date. All Counsel agree to the above. The Court clarified that the previously signed accounting order only addresses the issue of Deanna's commissions going to Inheritance Funding.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Verified Ex Parte Application Of Monique Mervin And Deanna A Boyd-Colon Co-Executors Of The Estate For Authority To Associate And Retain Litigation Counsel And Order Thereon
Off Calendar Jul-03-2013 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Heather Volante for Monique Mervin and Deanna Boyd-Colon. Steve Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to whether or not Deanna Boyd-Colon signed the assignment in the capacity of a fiduciary or an individual, and whether any of the Assignors had a beneficial interest in the Estate. Mr. Schroeder stated that there is a fundamental factual dispute, that Inheritance Funding was lead to believe that the properties were to be sold in the Estate and requested an evidentiary hearing. The Court granted Mr. Schroeder's request and set an evidentiary hearing on the issue of the assignments for two half days September 13, 2013 and September 20, 2013 both at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Trial briefs are due by August 9, 2013. Responses are due by August 16, 2013. Replies are due by August 23, 2013. The Parties are ordered to meet and confer to try and settle the matter. Status on settlement efforts was set for July 31, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered Deanna's commissions as Co-Executor be paid to Inheritance Funding. The accounting petition was taken under submission for the Court to consider the extraordinary fee request.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-22-2013 Continued To Jun-19-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Heather Volante for Monique Mervin and Deanna Boyd Colon (Co-Trustees). Steve Schroeder for Inheritance Funding Company. Douglas Lloyd (President of Inheritance Funding) also present.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Inheritance Funding's claims being paid/not paid out of probate and whether their assignment includes Deanna's commissions as Personal Representative of the Estate. The Petition for Final Distribution was continued to June 19, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Schroeder to provide further briefing regarding authority to have the claims paid out of probate. Mr. Schroeder's brief is to be filed by June 10, 2013. Ms. Volante's response is to be filed by June 14, 2013. Ms. Volante's extraordinary fees were not addressed.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-08-2013 Continued To May-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Heather Volante for Monique Mervin and Deanna Boyd-Colon.

Ms. Volante stated that she received a check for two years worth of delinquent rents, that she filed a Petition for Final Distribution yesterday and that the issue of bill board rents is the responsibility of the Successor Trustee. In addition, Ms. Volante stated that the 2008 Internal Revenue Service ("IRS") lien was resolved in 2010 but that they Trustee has been unable to get the IRS to release the lien and that the Trustees reserve the right to assign this issue to themselves. Status on filing of the Petition for Final Distribution was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy Saxen.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-19-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Heather Volante for Monique Mervin and Deanna Boyd Colon.

Ms. Volante explained circumstances with the properties, difficulty with obtaining information and records from the Successor Trustee of the Mother's Trust, spoke about the Successor Trustees $16,000.00 claim and request for credit towards rents, the $90,000.00 advance by Inheritance Funding, the Successor Trustee's two adult Children living in two of the properties without paying rents, property taxes and personal income taxes not having been paid, etc. In addition, Ms. Volante stated that she is willing to file a Petition for Final Distribution in the Estate, continue to negotiate with the Successor Trustee, that she was trying to build a pattern of cooperation with him and that she would like to move everything to the Trust. Ms. Volante also spoke of extraordinary fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission. The Court advised Ms. Volante that it probably would not approve 50% of the statutory fees. Status on filing of the Petition for Final Distribution was set for February 26, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Nov-28-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Heather Volante for Monique Mervin and Deanna Colon (Co-Executors).

The sale was confirmed at $309,750.00 for a 75% interest of the real property based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of 75% of the Real Property (order for the same signed in open Court). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
